proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=97, 93.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +42.3pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8690 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)

Regime is shifting (day 3 in Bull, prior week mixed Choppy/Bull with only 1 Bear day); if Bear re-emerges the cohort will activate into a portfolio already 94% UP_TRI-weighted, so watch UP_TRI signal-type concentration on re-activation
R-multiple 0.61 remains under D-8 review per project_anchor §7; monitor for Day-6 forced-exit dominance affecting realized edge
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=97, wr=93.7%, edge +42.3pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=97, Wilson lower 0.869, +42.3pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61 below the Tier S threshold. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Bull (shifting, day 3), so the cohort is dormant and no concentration trigger is currently active (observed 0.5545 below 0.80). Consistent with continuous prior approvals (2026-04-29 through 2026-07-03) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: BULL_PROXY × Bear: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=BULL_PROXY, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +29.9pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.98 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
R-multiple 0.98 remains just below Tier S threshold; a small downward drift or Day-6 forced-exit effect (project_anchor §7 D-8) could tip the cohort toward demotion on next archive comparison
approval would propagate redundant to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +29.9pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +29.9pp edge over BULL_PROXY baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.98 (<1.0). Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Bull (shifting, day 3), so the cohort is dormant; portfolio Bear exposure is only 11/101 (10.9%) and no regime concentration is triggered (0.5545 below 0.80), so promotion does not compound concentration risk and may diversify when Bear re-emerges. Consistent with continuous prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-06-19 through 07-03) with no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_signal_type = UP_TRI (94.1%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_signal_type = UP_TRI; recommended action: Pause new entries in signal_type=UP_TRI until directional monoculture drops below 85% via natural Day 6 exits; prefer DOWN_TRI or hedged entries to rebalance directional exposure
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: A Bull→Bear or sharp Choppy flip would unwind the long-heavy book simultaneously across 95 UP_TRI positions with no validated cohort edge (Candidate tier n=4) to justify holding through drawdown — correlated exits at scale.

evidence: Book is directional long: UP_TRI + BULL_PROXY = 98/101 = 97.03%
Top cell UP_TRI×Bull×Bank at 11.88% (12/101) — below 0.20 cell threshold but matched cohort Bull×UP_TRI is Candidate tier (n=4), unvalidated edge
Regime split Bull 55.45% / Choppy 33.66% / Bear 10.89% — regime concentration not triggered but mix is unstable
EOD archive missing; fallback source used (signal_history filter) — exposure counts derived, not authoritative
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new entries in signal_type=UP_TRI until directional monoculture drops below 85% via natural Day 6 exits; prefer DOWN_TRI or hedged entries to rebalance directional exposure
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: A Bull→Bear or sharp Choppy flip would unwind the long-heavy book simultaneously across 95 UP_TRI positions with no validated cohort edge (Candidate tier n=4) to justify holding through drawdown — correlated exits at scale.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: UP_TRI dominates the signal mix at 95/101 = 94.1% (well above the 0.85 threshold), making the book a near-monoculture directional long. The matched Bull×UP_TRI cohort for the top cell sits only at Candidate tier (n=4) — unvalidated edge. While regime concentration (Bull 55.45%) and the top cell (UP_TRI×Bull×Bank at 11.88%) are within their respective thresholds, the signal-type monoculture on an unvalidated cohort is the actionable risk.
